title: canarium indicum var. indicum
intro: Canarium indicum var. indicum, also known as the Indian almond or kachang buah, is a plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels and improving cardiovascular health. Canarium indicum var. indicum can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
